turns.content: one of the other vets just got an isuzu d-max and wont shut up about it. keeps telling me I should swap the hilux. need to know if hes actually right or if im justified in ignoring him.
The D-Max vs Hilux debate is a classic. What's his main argument for the Isuzu?
says the payload is better, the towing capacity is better, and its cheaper. which I think is three arguments but hes counting it as one big argument. also says the ride is smoother.

He's not entirely wrong on the specs -- the D-Max does edge out the Hilux on payload and towing on paper. And it is a bit cheaper. But the Hilux has a reputation for reliability that the D-Max doesn't quite match. What year is your Hilux?
its a 2025. not had a single issue with it. starts every morning, goes through anything, doesnt complain. which is more than I can say for most of my colleagues. human and animal.
Ha. A 2025 with no issues -- you'd be mad to swap it this early. The thing about Toyota is that boring reliability. It just works. How does it handle on the farm tracks?

aye its grand. not as good off road as my old vehicle was tbh but thats a high bar. the hilux handles the tracks fine, its just a bit wider so some of the tighter gates are a squeeze. but it gets through. and crucially the heater works which is a luxury I never had before.
Working heating in a Scottish winter is basically a premium feature. The width thing is a fair point though -- pickups have got bigger over the years. Does the D-Max have the same issue?

prob worse actually. his looks massive. like a vehicle thats been to the gym. all the new pickups are just getting wider and taller for no reason. the farmers up here have gates from the 1940s. nobody is making them bigger to accommodate our midlife crisis trucks.

"Midlife crisis trucks" -- accurate for a lot of pickup buyers, though I assume yours is genuinely for work. The gate issue is a real practical consideration that spec sheets don't cover. What about the inside -- is there enough space for all your vet kit?

aye the bed is fine for kit. ive got a canopy on it so everything stays dry. got the drugs box bolted in, calving ropes, the scanner, all the usual stuff. it works. thats all I need from a vehicle. it works and it starts.
Sounds like you've got it set up exactly how you need it. "It works and it starts" is a pretty low bar, but I suspect that's born from past experience with vehicles that didn't.
aye lets just say ive had vehicles where starting was more of a suggestion than a guarantee. those days are behind me. the hilux just turns on like a normal machine. revolutionary concept.
The bar was underground and Toyota cleared it. So what are you going to tell your colleague -- staying with the Hilux?

ill tell him his d-max is very nice and then continue driving my hilux until it dies. which based on toyota reliability will be roughly 2045. by which point ill be too old to care about trucks. perfect plan.
A twenty-year plan built entirely around not having to think about vehicles again. That's peak pragmatism. I think you've made the right call.
aye. right cheers for confirming what I already knew. just needed external validation. ta.
Anytime. External validation is what I'm here for. Enjoy the Hilux.
